info.title: ModelOps Admin API
info.description: Admin APIs play a foundational role in the ModelOps lifecycle by enabling the onboarding, configuration, and governance of tenants, users, and AI models. They serve as the entry point for initializing environments, managing access, and ensuring operational readiness for model deployment and usage.

servers.description: Access Token for Admin Credentials API

post.description: ModelOps Admin APIs require secure access via user-level authentication. Users must first obtain an access token using the https://cognito-idp.amazonaws.com/ endpoint.

errorMessage.example: Incorrect username or password / User pool client xxxxx does not exist.

post.description:
Use this API endpoint to onboard a tenant.
Request Body Properties Definition Data Type Required/Optional
name Name of the tenant string Required
emailId Maximus email address of the tenant string Required
dataRetentionPeriodInDays number of days the data is stored.(default value is 1) integer Optional
credentialsCount Number of credentials needed for the particular tenant (default value is 1) integer Optional
dispatchType Type of dispatch mechanism (Step_Function, SNS, Event_Bridge, URL) string Optional
dispatchConfig Configuration object for the chosen dispatch type. string Optional

dispatchType.description: Type of notification dispatch to use for the tenant.

dispatchConfig.description: Configuration object whose required fields depend on dispatchType.

errorMessage.example: An error occured while onboarding tenant : xxxx already exists

get.description: This API allows ModelOps administrators to know the details of an existing tenant.

errorMessage.example: Tenant does not exist for given Id - xxxxx

patch.description:
This API allows ModelOps administrators to update the details of an existing tenant. Typical use cases include modifying the tenant name, updating associated models, or changing metadata.
Request Body Properties Definition Data Type Required/Optional
emailId Email address that is going to show after updation string Conditional
rateLimit Rate limit that is needed for the tenant integer (also accepts numeric strings, e.g., "1000") Conditional
burstLimit Burst limit that is needed for the tenant integer (also accepts numeric strings, e.g., "1000") Conditional
quotaLimit Quota limit that is needed for the tenant integer (also accepts numeric strings, e.g., "1000") Conditional
quotaPeriod Quota period that is needed for the tenant. can be DAY, WEEK, and MONTH. string Conditional
dataRetentionPeriodInDays Data retaintion needed for the tenant in days. integer Conditional
dispatchType Type of dispatch mechanism (Step_Function, SNS, Event_Bridge, URL) string Conditional
dispatchConfig Configuration object for the chosen dispatch type. string Conditional

quotaLimit.description: Total number of requests allowed in the quota period

dispatchType.description: Type of notification dispatch to use for the tenant.

dispatchConfig.description: Configuration object whose required fields depend on dispatchType.

oneOf.description: No dispatch changes in this request.

UpdateEmailAndDispatchSNS.summary: Update email and set SNS dispatch

UseDirectUrlCallback.summary: Send callbacks to a direct URL

errorMessage.example: An error occured while updating tenant details : Period should be one of DAY,MONTH or WEEK

delete.description: This API allows ModelOps administrators to deboard an existing tenant.

errorMessage.example: An error occured while deboarding tenant : xxxxx does not exist

patch.description:
This API allows ModelOps administrators to rotate client secrets or revoke credentials for a tenant. It is essential for maintaining security when credentials are compromised, lost, or need to be refreshed.
Request Body Properties Definition Data Type Required/Optional
tenantId Unique identifier of the tenant whose credentials are being updated. string Required
clientIds List of client IDs whose credentials are to be rotated or revoked array of strings Required
rotateClientSecret Set to true to rotate the client secret for the specified client IDs. boolean Conditional
revokeCredentials Set to true to revoke credentials for the specified client IDs. boolean Conditional

errorMessage.example: An error occured while updating tenant credentials : clientIds must be a list
An error occured while updating tenant credentials : No credentials found for tenantId=xxxxx

post.summary: Associating a Tenant with a Model API
post.description:
Use it to associate an available model with the tenant.
Request Body Properties Definition Data Type Required/Optional
modelAccess List of model access configurations for the tenant. Each object defines access details for a specific model. array of objects Required
modelID Unique identifier of the AI model to be onboarded string Required
access Access control action.Use "grant" to allow access to the model. Use "revoke" to remove access to the model. string Required
modelAccess Access type. Use "shared" to indicate shared model access across tenants. Use "dedicated" to indicated dedicated model acess across tenants string Required
tenantID ID of the tenant to which the model is being onboarded or deboarded string Required

errorMessage.example: Invalid access value xxxx . Must be one of ['grant', 'revoke']
Model not exist in modelops for the give id -xxxxxx

post.description: This API allows ModelOps administrators to onboard a model.

delete.description: This API allows ModelOps administrators to deboard a model.

patch.description: This API allows ModelOps administrators to update the maxToken of a model.

errorMessage.example: Max tokens must be between 1 and 4096 for amazon.titan-text-lite-v1.

post.description:
Allows ModelOps administrators to onboard a user.
Request Body Properties Definition Data Type Required/Optional
emailId Email ID of the user string Required
firstName First name of the user string Required
lastName Surname of user string Optional

errorMessage.example: User with email xxxx@maximus.com already exists.

post.summary: Associating or Disassociating a User with a Tenant API
post.description:
This API allows ModelOps administrators to associate or disassociate a user with a tenant.
Request Body Properties Definition Data Type Required/Optional
emailId Maximus email address of the user string Required
tenantId ID of the tenant to which the user is being onboarded or deboarded. string Required
accessType Access type. Use "grant" to allow the user to associate with the tenantId, use "revoke" to disassociate the user from the mentioned tenantId string Required

errorMessage.example: Tenant not found for the provided id - xxxx

post.description: Use this API endpoint to onboard a workflow into ModelOps. This registers the workflow name(workflow_id), dispatch type, and dispatch configuration.

errorMessage.example: Workflow name 'xxx' already exists. Please choose a unique name.

post.summary: Associating and disassociating tenants with a workflow API
post.description: Use this API to grant or revoke access between tenants and workflows in ModelOps. This endpoint supports bulk operations, allowing you to associate or disassociate multiple tenants and workflows in a single request.

post.summary: Onboarding and deboarding a model to the workflow API
post.description: Use this API to grant or revoke access between models and workflows in ModelOps. This endpoint supports bulk operations, allowing you to associate or disassociate multiple models and workflows in a single request.

errorMessage.example: Workflow Id xxxx does not exists in modelops

patch.description: Use this API endpoint to update a workflow that is already onboarded to ModelOps. User can update the dispatchType and dispatchConfig using this API.

parameters.name: workflow_id
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow to update

errorMessage.example: No workflow found for workflowId: xxxx

delete.description: Use this API endpoint to deboard a workflow from ModelOps.

parameters.name: workflow_id
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow to delete

errorMessage.example: No workflow found for workflow_name: xxxx

dispatchType.description: Dispatch mechanism configured for the workflow

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the workflow dispatch settings were last updated
